title: The Kanban Guide
description: Definitions, practices, metrics, and flow concepts from The Kanban Guide.

questions.question: What is the definitive definition of Kanban according to the guide?
answers: A framework that mandates 30-day timeboxes.
A specific software tool used to track individual tasks.
A strategy for optimizing the flow of value through a process.
A method for estimating work effort using story points.
questions.explanation: The guide defines Kanban as a strategy for optimizing the flow of value through a process.

questions.question: How many core practices comprise Kanban when working in tandem?
answers: One
Two
Three
Five
questions.explanation: Kanban is comprised of three practices working in tandem.

questions.question: In a Kanban system, what is the definition of Flow?
answers: The speed at which developers write code.
The number of hours a team spends in meetings.
The movement of potential value through a system.
The hierarchy of management in an organization.
questions.explanation: Flow is the movement of potential value through a system.

questions.question: What are the three elements of value optimization that Kanban system members strive to balance?
answers: Speed, Cost, and Quality.
Scope, Schedule, and Budget.
Effectiveness, Efficiency, and Predictability.
Planning, Execution, and Review.
questions.explanation: Kanban system members strive to balance effectiveness, efficiency, and predictability.

questions.question: Which of the following is one of the three core practices of Kanban?
answers: Sprint Planning.
Actively managing items in a workflow.
Estimating relative complexity.
Assigning tasks to specific individuals.
questions.explanation: Actively managing items in a workflow is one of the three Kanban practices.

questions.question: What is a Work Item in the context of Kanban?
answers: A specific person assigned to a task.
An individual unit of value moving through the workflow.
The physical board used to track progress.
A meeting held to discuss process improvements.
questions.explanation: A work item is an individual unit of value moving through the workflow.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a Kanban board?
answers: To track the billable hours of each employee.
To provide a list of all ideas that will never be worked on.
To visualize the Definition of Workflow (DoW) and make it transparent.
To replace the need for team communication.
questions.explanation: A Kanban board visualizes the Definition of Workflow and makes it transparent.

questions.question: Which metric measures the number of work items finished per unit of time?
answers: Work in Progress (WIP).
Throughput.
Cycle Time.
Work Item Age.
questions.explanation: Throughput measures the number of work items finished per unit of time.

questions.question: How is Work In Progress (WIP) defined?
answers: The total number of items in the backlog.
The number of work items started but not finished.
The number of items finished in the last month.
The average time it takes to complete one task.
questions.explanation: WIP is the number of work items started but not finished.

questions.question: What does Cycle Time represent?
answers: The time a work item spends in the backlog.
The elapsed time between when a work item started and when it finished.
The time a team spends in the active working state only.
The time between two consecutive releases.
questions.explanation: Cycle Time is the elapsed time between when a work item started and when it finished.

questions.question: Which metric is defined as the elapsed time between when a work item started and the current date?
answers: Lead Time.
Throughput.
Work Item Age.
Flow Efficiency.
questions.explanation: Work Item Age is the elapsed time between when a work item started and the current date.

questions.question: What are the two required components of a Service Level Expectation (SLE)?
answers: A start date and a finish date.
A period of elapsed time and an associated probability.
A list of tasks and an assigned resource.
A story point estimate and a confidence interval.
questions.explanation: An SLE has a period of elapsed time and an associated probability.

questions.question: What is a fundamental requirement for a Definition of Workflow (DoW)?
answers: It must be approved by an external auditor.
It must include a list of team members' names.
It must include a definition of how WIP will be controlled.
It must be updated exactly every 30 days.
questions.explanation: A Definition of Workflow must include a definition of how WIP will be controlled.

questions.question: In a Kanban Pull System, when should members start work on a new item?
answers: Whenever a manager requests it.
As soon as an item is added to the backlog.
Only when there is a clear signal that there is capacity to do so.
At the beginning of every week.
questions.explanation: Members start new work only when there is a clear signal that capacity is available.

questions.question: What is the guide's advice regarding Work Item Age during active management?
answers: Age is irrelevant as long as the item eventually finishes.
Members should ensure work items do not age unnecessarily, using the SLE as a reference.
Age should only be calculated for items that have already finished.
Older items should be discarded immediately without review.
questions.explanation: Members should ensure work items do not age unnecessarily, using the SLE as a reference.

questions.question: When should improvements to a workflow be implemented?
answers: Only during a formal monthly retrospective.
Only when mandated by a corporate executive.
Just-in-time as the context dictates, without needing to wait for a formal meeting.
Only after at least one year of data collection.
questions.explanation: Workflow improvements are made just-in-time as the context dictates.
